div1.entryFree:
Unwholesome, 1) not sound, diseased; and in a moral sense, == tainted, impaired, defective: this u. humidity (viz Falstaff) Wiv. III, 3, 42. they're too u. Per. IV, 2, 22. the people muddied, thick and u. in their thoughts and whispers, Hml. IV, 5, 82. bear some charity to my wit; do not think it so u. Oth. IV, 1, 125.
2) unfavourable to health, insalubrious: Lucr. 779. Lucr. 779 Tp. I, 2, 322. H5 II, 3, 59. Troil. II, 3, 129. Cor. IV, 6, 130.
